summary: The available literature supports the presence of established surgical frameworks encompassing endoscopic and retroperitoneal procedures, which imply existing methods for teaching and practicing retroperitoneoscopic nephrectomy despite no explicit reference to this specific technique. Structured taxonomies in urology suggest viable educational models are implicitly supported by current procedural classifications.
